senses.gloss: October (tenth month of the Gregorian and Julian calendars); only used in تِشْرِين الْأَوَّل (tišrīn al-ʔawwal, “October”) or in ellipses thereof
October (tenth month of the Gregorian and Julian calendars); only used in تِشْرِين الثَّانِي (tišrīn aṯ-ṯānī, “November”)

etymology.text: From Classical Syriac ܬܫܪܝܢ (tišrīn, “autumn month”), ultimately from Akkadian 𒌚𒇯 (tišritum), and cognate with Hebrew תִּשְׁרֵי (tišrei, “Tishrei, the seventh month of the Hebrew lunisolar calendar”).
